rows_detail.boundary: Do not imply hardware routing, native-gate scheduling, noise modeling, or device execution.
rows_detail.item: Logical-layer scope visible at first screening
rows_detail.risk: Editor may reject the manuscript as an overbroad hardware compiler claim.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The manuscript can be sent to reviewers as a logical-layer oracle-synthesis paper.

rows_detail.boundary: The claim is not universal superiority over every synthesis method.
rows_detail.risk: Editor may see the work as only an SSHR variant or as a weak-baseline comparison.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The screening package explains why SSHR is a baseline and why comparisons are layered.

rows_detail.boundary: Role labels clarify the current evidence; they do not broaden any comparison beyond the logical-layer claim.
rows_detail.risk: Editor or reviewer may treat controls, probes, and counterpoints as if they were all primary benchmarks.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The package makes comparison roles explicit before the results are read as a universal leaderboard.

rows_detail.boundary: Weighted-score wins must stay separated from raw resource dominance.
rows_detail.risk: Reviewer may object that weighted-score wins hide CNOT, depth, or ancilla losses.
rows_detail.supported_decision: Unfavorable metric evidence is visible before review rather than hidden in raw tables.

rows_detail.boundary: The largest gains remain tied to algebraic action space and guarded/Pareto selection.
rows_detail.risk: Reviewer may reject an overclaim that deep learning alone explains the gains.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The manuscript can claim neural/MCTS search-control support without overclaiming deep RL.

rows_detail.boundary: Do not claim exhaustive high-dimensional truth-table benchmarking.
rows_detail.risk: Reviewer may object that the n=48--64 symbolic stress rows are not exhaustive truth-table checks.
rows_detail.supported_decision: Large-scale evidence is framed as symbolic verification plus bounded truth-table bridges.

rows_detail.boundary: The lightweight rebuild verifies paper-facing outputs; raw sweeps remain heavier rerun tiers.
rows_detail.risk: Editor may desk-reject if the manuscript cannot be audited from submitted artifacts.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The submission package exposes a clear reviewer path from claims to files and scripts.

rows_detail.boundary: Author order, affiliations, funding, conflicts, venue choice, and archive links still require author input.
rows_detail.risk: Submission may be blocked by missing declarations, anonymous-review policy, or archive links.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The remaining nontechnical submission gate is explicit and private rather than implicit.

rows_detail.boundary: This is a navigation aid, not an acceptance guarantee.
rows_detail.risk: Editor may miss the comparison boundary if the package lacks a short triage route.
rows_detail.supported_decision: The support package gives editors a concise route to judge fit before full review.
